# function: geohash_encode

Returns the [geohash](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geohash) of the given `(latitude, longitude)` at the requested `precision` (number of base-32 characters). Higher precision = smaller cell. Approximate cell side at the equator (worst case):

| precision | cell side |
| ---: | --- |
| 1 | ~5,000 km |
| 3 | ~156 km |
| 5 | ~4.9 km |
| 7 | ~152 m |
| 9 | ~4.8 m |
| 12 | ~3.7 cm |

`precision` must be in `[1, 12]`. `latitude` must be in `[-90, 90]` and `longitude` in `[-180, 180]`.

```
geohash_encode(37.7749, -122.4194, 7)
→ "9q8yyk8"   (≈ Civic Center, San Francisco)
```

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Encode (latitude, longitude) into a geohash. Higher precision narrows the cell ten-fold per character.
output "civic_center_sf" {
  value = provider::burnham::geohash_encode(37.7749, -122.4194, 7)
  // → "9q8yyk8"  (≈ 152 m × 152 m)
}

output "country_scale" {
  value = provider::burnham::geohash_encode(37.7749, -122.4194, 3)
  // → "9q8"      (≈ 156 km × 156 km)
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
geohash_encode(latitude number, longitude number, precision number) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `latitude` (Number) Latitude in degrees, [-90, 90].
1. `longitude` (Number) Longitude in degrees, [-180, 180].
1. `precision` (Number) Number of base-32 characters in the resulting hash; in [1, 12].
